results.Title: Cabin Safety Event
results.Occurrence:
During cruise at flight level FL410, the PF observed an engine indication and crew alerting system (EICAS) status message on the multifunction display (MFD) “Cabin Air CPRSR R” associated with cabin air compressor (CAC) failure. The PM referred to the quick reference handbook (QRH) that directs to descend to 35,000 feet for a subsequent pack reset.  
After resetting the air conditioning system, another alert message appeared indicating that the left pressurization air conditioning kit (PACK) failed whereas the right PACK was operating with one CAC. 
After  few minutes, an indication of the right PACK failure appeared and consequently, the PF decided to descend to 10,000 feet, based on the QRH checklist, with the activation of oxygen masks in the cabin.
